message:
perf(lexical/cass-compat): eliminate intermediate Vec in par_chunks doc batching; add writer-parallelism-aware open_or_create

Batched document ingestion previously materialized each rayon chunk into a
Vec<TantivyDocument> before handing the prepared docs to the Tantivy writer.
The intermediate Vec was wasted work: `writer.run` already consumes an
Iterator<Item = UserOperation>, so the collect() step was just allocating
and freeing a vector per chunk on a hot ingestion path.

This change streams the `build_cass_tantivy_document` (and `_ref` sibling)
construction directly into `UserOperation::Add` via iterator adapters,
which keeps the per-chunk overhead bounded by the Tantivy writer's own
internal buffering instead of an extra O(batch_docs) allocation. Applied
symmetrically to both `add_cass_documents` and `add_cass_document_refs`
so owned and borrowed callers see the same improvement.

Separately, introduce
  `CassTantivyIndex::open_or_create_with_writer_parallelism(path, n)`
as a sibling to `open_or_create(path)`. The existing entry point still
probes `std::thread::available_parallelism()` and forwards to the new
method, preserving default behavior for all current callers.

Motivation: staged cass shard builds want to open multiple isolated
shard writers concurrently. Without an explicit budget, each writer
claimed the machine-wide thread count, oversubscribing the box. The new
constructor threads an explicit parallelism value through to
`cass_writer_config_for_parallelism`, which was already wired up for
the internal writer-config path and the unit tests (see the matrix at
sizes 1/2/4/8/32/64 in cass_compat.rs).

No behavioral change for single-writer callers. No schema / on-disk
format change; the writer config only influences in-process indexing
thread count and heap sizing.
